notes: Multi-factor unlocking via an arbitrary monotone boolean access policy (AND/OR) over named factors: passwords + FIDO2 YubiKeys. Construction: one random DEK encrypts the vault; the DEK is secret-shared down the policy formula tree (OR replicates the node secret, AND XOR-splits it); each leaf share is wrapped with its factor KEK (password = Argon2id, yubikey = HKDF of the FIDO2 hmac-secret). Factors and policy are managed in-store while unlocked; interactive init prompts for a password and offers a YubiKey. Ships as 0.3.0 behind a yubikey cargo feature. Security: OR strength equals the weakest satisfying set, so password branches must stay strong.
title: YubiKey + multi-factor auth policy (0.3.0)

notes: New CypherVersion for policy-protected vaults. Header: version, flags, iv, pad_len. Body: the policy formula tree (AND/OR nodes + factor-leaf references), a table of leaves each holding factor params + wrapped share, then the DEK-encrypted payload + HMAC. Old V7 password-direct vaults keep opening. Pick a serialization (bincode like the existing header, or small TLV) that stays forward-extensible for new factor types.
title: Design v8 keyslot+policy file format

notes: Monotone boolean access structure. Tree: And(children), Or(children), Leaf(factor_id). distribute(secret, tree): OR copies the node secret to every child; AND XOR-splits into one random share per child (s1 xor ... xor sn = secret); leaves get their assigned share. reconstruct(tree, provided): leaf yields its share if the factor was provided; OR yields any present child; AND yields the XOR of all children iff all present; root yields the DEK iff satisfied. Exhaustively unit-test every satisfying and non-satisfying factor subset for representative policies including pass1 or (pass2 and yk1).
title: Policy secret-sharing engine

notes: Generate a random DEK (32B key + 32B HMAC key) at vault creation; the DEK encrypts the payload via the existing Cypher envelope. Wrap and unwrap each leaf share by reusing Cypher::new(KEK).encrypt/decrypt, whose HMAC check signals a satisfied factor. Wire the DEK and wrapped leaves into the new format.
title: DEK key hierarchy + leaf wrap via Cypher

notes: Password factor: per-factor random salt + Argon2id params; KEK = Argon2id(password, salt), reusing the existing derivation. Store the factor params in the leaf. Memory-hard because the input is a human password.
title: Password factor (Argon2id KEK)

notes: Parse a boolean expression over factor names into the formula tree, e.g. pass1 or (pass2 and yk-main). Grammar: factor names, and, or, parentheses; and binds tighter than or; recursive descent. Validate that all referenced factors exist. Render a tree back to a canonical expression for policy show.
title: Policy expression parser

notes: Enroll and remove named factors; set and show the policy. On any change, re-run the DEK distribution for the new tree and rewrite the leaves; the DEK is unchanged so the payload is not re-encrypted. Requires being unlocked (DEK in hand), like LUKS keyslot management. Persist factors, policy, and wrapped leaves into the new format.
title: Factor and policy management

notes: Add interactive in-store commands to InteractiveCli, run while unlocked, driving factor and policy management: enroll password NAME; enroll yubikey NAME with optional pin; factors; remove factor NAME; policy show; policy set EXPR. Update the help text.
title: In-store auth commands

notes: New-store creation flow: prompt for a password (factor pass1), then offer to enroll a YubiKey now; if yes, run FIDO2 enrollment and ask whether both factors are required or either (and/or) to set the initial policy. Default policy with a single password is just that factor.
title: Interactive new-store init flow

notes: On open, read the policy and prompt for a satisfying set of factors: password entry for password factors, touch plus optional PIN for yubikey factors, collecting opportunistically until the root is satisfied. Reconstruct the DEK and build the Cypher from it. Handle wrong-factor and unsatisfiable cases gracefully.
title: Policy-driven unlock UX

notes: Implement the yubikey factor with ctap-hid-fido2 behind a yubikey cargo feature. Enroll: make_credential with the hmac-secret extension (non-resident), an rp_id such as rcypher, store credential_id + per-factor salt + uv_required. Unlock: get_assertion with the credential and salt, user-presence true (touch), user-verification optional (PIN); KEK = HKDF of the hmac-secret output (no Argon2, the secret is high-entropy). Verify ctap-hid-fido2 supports hmac-secret. Hardware-validated by the maintainer.
title: FIDO2 YubiKey factor (hmac-secret)

notes: Warn when a policy has a satisfying set reachable by a single password factor alone (or otherwise weak), because OR security equals the weakest satisfying set. Surface at policy set time and on store open. Nice-to-have security UX.
title: Warn on weak policy branches

notes: Document the policy model (DEK plus formula-tree secret sharing), the in-store commands, FIDO2 enrollment, recovery and backup guidance (keep a strong recovery password branch and enroll a backup YubiKey as another factor), and the weakest-satisfying-set caveat. Update README, security docs, and CHANGELOG.
title: Docs for the auth/policy model

notes: Bump to 0.3.0 and follow docs/crate_release_process.md (publish rcypher then rcypher-cli; release.yml builds the four binaries). The yubikey feature is additive; confirm the default build, the lean core, and the macOS and ARM cross-checks stay green.
title: Release 0.3.0

notes: The secret-sharing path passes shares and recovered authkeys through plain Vec<u8>: keyslot::unwrap_share copies out of a Zeroizing buffer via to_vec; policy::reconstruct accumulates Vec<u8> shares; vault unlock builds a Vec<Option<Vec<u8>>>. The final DEK and the authkek map entries ARE Zeroizing, but these transient 64-byte secret buffers are not wiped. Thread Zeroizing through unwrap_share, reconstruct's provided/accumulator, and to_key_material's source so no share/authkek lingers un-zeroized. Matches rcypher zeroize-everything ethos. Deferred from yk-factor-mgmt to keep that task focused on the architecture.
title: Tighten zeroization of policy shares/authkeks

notes: Done so far: docs/auth-protocol.md — the normative multi-factor protocol spec (DEK/auth-KEK/share, wrap/unwrap with the AES-KW-vs-AEAD caveat, every operation step by step, worked example, security properties, and literature citations incl. Benaloh-Leichter monotone secret sharing). Remaining: README 'Use as a library'/security sections, CHANGELOG, the in-store command reference, recovery/backup guidance.

CLI wiring complete. Added Backend enum (rcypher-cli/src/cli/backend.rs) abstracting Legacy(v7 Cypher) vs Policy(v8 PolicyVault). main.rs detects v8 via is_policy_vault() (2-byte version probe) before the shared password prompt and runs run_interactive_policy: shows policy expr, collects a satisfying factor set opportunistically (PolicyNode::is_satisfied_by, skip-on-empty), opens via PolicyVault::open, builds the DEK-keyed Cypher. Wrong-factor and unsatisfiable cases error out. Lib additions: PolicyNode::is_satisfied_by, PolicyMetadata::policy_expr. Integration tests: test_policy_vault_put_get_roundtrip, test_policy_vault_wrong_password_fails.

The 'enroll password NAME' command prompts via rpassword (reads /dev/tty), so it cannot be driven through assert_cmd stdin. Underlying vault.enroll_password is unit-tested, and the CLI wiring (parse/save/print) is thin. Add an end-to-end PTY-based test (e.g. via a pty crate) that enrolls a second password factor, sets a policy referencing it, and unlocks via the new factor. Deferred test debt from yk-interactive-cmds.
title: PTY test for in-store enroll password

notes: Done. Added in-store auth commands to InteractiveCli (run() and process_cmd() now &mut self): 'factors', 'enroll password NAME', 'policy show', 'policy set EXPR', 'remove factor NAME'; help text updated. Commands operate on Backend::Policy via new policy_vault()/policy_vault_mut() accessors and bail clearly on legacy stores. Mutations (enroll/set-policy/remove) re-save the store (new metadata) with the DEK unchanged. InteractiveCli now carries argon2_params for enrolling new factors. Integration tests cover factors/policy show/policy set/remove factor and legacy rejection; enroll's TTY password prompt deferred to [[yk-enroll-pty-test]].

Done (infrastructure). New stores are created as version-8 policy vaults with a single 'password' factor via main.rs create_backend(); existing v7 stores still open via the legacy path. Unified store acquisition: open_backend() (v8 unlock or v7 legacy) + acquire_store_backend() (open-or-create). update-with generalised to merge two unlocked Backends (legacy or policy), so it works with the new default. The interactive YubiKey-enrollment offer (and the and/or initial-policy choice) is deferred to [[yk-fido2-factor]] since it needs the FIDO2 factor; users can already add factors to a new store via the in-store enroll/policy commands.

Also wire the new-store init-flow YubiKey offer here: after create_backend() enrolls the password factor, offer 'enroll a YubiKey now?'; if yes, run FIDO2 enrollment and ask whether both factors are required (AND) or either (OR) to set the initial policy. The enrollment plumbing (enroll yubikey command, FactorKind::Yubikey, collect_policy_secrets YubiKey branch) all converge here.

Done. PolicyNode::referenced_factors() + PolicyMetadata::single_password_unlockers() (lib); CLI warns on stderr at 'policy set' and on store open via utils::warn_single_password_unlock. Baseline single-password stores are not flagged (guarded by referenced_factors().len() < 2). Unit + integration tested.

Done. Added policy::Share = Zeroizing<Vec<u8>> and threaded it through unwrap_share (returns the Zeroizing buffer, no to_vec), distribute (Zeroizing shares + AND-split residual/random), reconstruct (provided + accumulator + return), and vault unlock/recover_all_authkeks into to_key_material. Share re-exported. All tests green.

Progress: README multi-factor section (policy model, in-store command reference, DEK/secret-sharing explainer, recovery/backup guidance, weakest-set caveat), file-format note (v8 keyslot header), roadmap refresh, and an Unreleased CHANGELOG entry are done. docs/auth-protocol.md already complete. Remaining (blocked on yk-fido2-factor): FIDO2 YubiKey enrollment walkthrough + the touch/PIN unlock UX docs.

Done. Added rcypher-cli/tests/enroll_pty.rs: spawns the binary under a real pseudo-terminal (rexpect dev-dep) and drives the full 'enroll password' TTY flow — answers the /dev/tty password+confirm prompts, sets a policy referencing the new factor, and asserts via the lib that the store then unlocks with the enrolled factor alone. Switched off the deprecated CommandCargoExt::cargo_bin to the cargo_bin! macro. Closes the only previously-untested CLI path.

The interactive session leaves the terminal in an unusable state (raw mode: no echo, broken line discipline) when it exits on the idle timeout or a security trip. Cause: the security-timer thread in main.rs start_security_timer() calls std::process::exit(0/1) directly (idle timeout, debugger detected, frozen clock, missed heartbeat). std::process::exit() does NOT run destructors, so rustyline's Editor Drop — which restores the terminal from raw mode — never runs. (The in-loop bail!() paths are fine: they return Err from run(), the Editor 'rl' is dropped on scope exit, and the terminal is restored.) Fix: restore the terminal before the timer thread exits the process. Options: (a) save the original termios at startup and tcsetattr() it back (plus emit a reset, e.g. newline / RIS) in the timer thread right before process::exit; (b) register a libc atexit handler that restores termios (process::exit runs C atexit handlers); or (c) have the timer thread signal the main thread to exit cleanly so the Editor drops normally (needs to interrupt a blocked rl.readline()). Option (a) or (b) is simplest and robust. Verify by letting the idle timeout fire while at the prompt and confirming the shell is still usable (echo on).
title: Restore terminal before exiting on timeout/security exit

notes: UPDATE (portability of the atexit option): atexit handlers DO run on std::process::exit (it calls C exit() on both Unix and Windows, unlike _exit), so that part is portable — BUT the restore body is not: termios/tcsetattr is POSIX-only, Windows would need SetConsoleMode. Moot here since rcypher is Unix-only (Linux+macOS, all nix/cfg(unix)-gated). The real catch with atexit: the C callback takes no args, so the saved termios must live in a global static (the arg-passing on_exit is non-portable — absent on macOS). PREFERRED FIX: option (a), not atexit — capture the original termios with nix::sys::termios::tcgetattr at startup (before rustyline enters raw mode), move a clone/Arc into the security-timer thread closure, and tcsetattr(TCSANOW) it back (plus emit a newline) right before each process::exit. No global, no atexit-ordering reliance, portable across Linux+macOS via nix.

Add an in-store 'upgrade' command that converts a legacy single-password (v7) store into a multi-factor policy vault (v8), so existing stores can use enroll/policy/YubiKey. Re-encrypt every value from the legacy key to a fresh random DEK; the entered password becomes the 'primary' factor (confirm + strength-check, like enroll). Swap the session backend Legacy->Policy and re-encrypt the in-memory storage too. Add a discoverable notice when a legacy store is opened, and an upgrade hint on the auth-command 'no policy' error. Also fix the stale README 'Upgrading storage format' section (it documents a removed pre-Argon2 upgrade + a nonexistent --upgrade-storage flag).
title: Upgrade legacy v7 store to a v8 policy vault

notes: Done. In-store 'upgrade' command (interactive.rs cmd_upgrade): legacy->policy vault, re-encrypts all values from the v7 key to a fresh DEK, password becomes 'primary' factor (confirm + strength check), swaps backend + in-memory storage. Legacy-open notice, auth-error upgrade hint, help + completer entries. DEFAULT_FACTOR_ID moved to cli/mod.rs. README/CHANGELOG fixed (the old section documented a removed pre-Argon2 upgrade + nonexistent --upgrade-storage flag). PTY tests for upgrade-with-value and upgrade-rejected-on-policy-vault.

REMOVED per user feedback: the weak-policy warning was reverted. The user confirmed a single strong password (or an OR branch reachable by one password) is an acceptable setup, so the nag was dropped — along with warn_single_password_unlock, PolicyMetadata::single_password_unlockers, and PolicyNode::referenced_factors. Per-password strength (zxcvbn) enforcement is kept; only the policy-level OR warning is gone.

Apply the verified, clippy-clean visibility narrowings from the dead-code/exports audit. Do just before publishing; review together. All confirmed zero external callers and each builds clean under the strict lint set (incl. redundant_pub_crate).

Narrowings:
- Cypher::version (crypto/cipher.rs) pub -> pub(crate) (only cipher.rs + storage/value.rs).
- Cypher::encrypt_with_aad / decrypt_with_aad (crypto/cipher.rs) pub -> pub(crate) (only auth/*).
- EncryptionKey::as_bytes / hmac_key / salt / version (crypto/key.rs) pub -> pub(super) (only cipher.rs).
- LimitedReader (struct + new, crypto/utils.rs) -> pub(super); drop the 'pub use utils::LimitedReader;' re-export in crypto/mod.rs and switch cipher.rs to 'use super::utils::LimitedReader;'.
- RingBuffer (struct + new/available/capacity/space_available/fill_from/read_exact, crypto/utils.rs) -> pub(super) (only stream_ops.rs).

Cannot narrow: cross-module 'pub use' re-exports (cypher_from_material, KeyMaterial, VaultHeader, PolicyVault, UnlockSession, FileContainerV8, etc.) must stay 'pub' - redundant_pub_crate forbids pub(crate) imports in private modules; already minimal.

Also re-check (added during the deep-review fix pass): visibility of the new helpers is_factor_id_char (auth/policy/parser.rs) and probe_u16_tag (version.rs), and whether the cfg(test) auth re-exports Leaf/PolicyNode are still needed after the leaf-iterator refactor. Confirm no dead code remains.
title: Tighten internal visibility before publishing

notes: Relocate the reusable interactive plumbing from rcypher-cli into the rcypher lib behind a 'cli' cargo feature, so other apps can build an rcypher-like CLI: password/PIN/touch prompts, zxcvbn strength check, and the policy authentication (unlock) loop. rcypher-cli becomes a thin shell. Pulls rpassword/zxcvbn behind the feature. Deferred from yk-fido2-factor (the FIDO2 factor itself ships first).
title: Reusable CLI plumbing behind a lib  feature
Reusable CLI plumbing behind a lib cli feature

notes:
The prebuilt static musl Linux release binaries currently omit FIDO2 (release.yml builds them with --no-default-features), because hidapi's default linux-static-hidraw backend needs libudev, which can't static-link into a musl binary. macOS prebuilts and cargo-install builds keep FIDO2.

Fix: switch hidapi to its pure-Rust 'linux-native-basic-udev' backend, which needs NO pkg-config / libudev / C-hidapi build (verified: it compiles with PKG_CONFIG=/bin/false). It reads /sys/class/hidraw directly. Then musl can ship FIDO2 and the rust.yml apt steps (pkg-config + libudev-dev) can be removed.

Mechanism: ctap-hid-fido2 hardcodes hidapi features=["linux-static-hidraw"] and hidapi panics if two backends are enabled, so feature-unification can't switch it. Must override via [patch.crates-io] ctap-hid-fido2 = { git=<fork> | path=<vendor> } pointing to a copy whose Cargo.toml uses linux-native-basic-udev (one-line change). Best: upstream a PR to ctap-hid-fido2 exposing backend selection, then no fork.

Caveats: [patch] does NOT propagate to crates.io, so it only fixes OUR workspace builds (CI/dev/release), not downstream lib consumers. ring still needs a C/asm toolchain (cc), present on runners. The native backend is less battle-tested than C hidapi — hardware-validate enrol+unlock before shipping.
title: Ship FIDO2 in static musl release binaries (pure-Rust HID backend)

notes: Done. Added the FIDO2 security-key enrollment walkthrough (the at-creation offer with the AND/OR policy choice, and the in-store `auth factor add fido2 NAME` transcript) and the touch/PIN unlock UX to the README, plus a PIN auto-detection + uv-mode-binding caveat (re-enrol after removing a PIN / enabling alwaysUv). Fixed the now-stale unlock paragraph (names are encrypted until unlock, so no policy/factor names are shown and matches report generically). Added the missing CHANGELOG 'FIDO2 security-key factor' entry and listed `add fido2 NAME` in the command reference. Generic FIDO2 naming throughout (no brand). docs/auth-protocol.md was already complete. Task complete.

Done. Relocated the reusable interactive plumbing into the rcypher lib behind a `cli` cargo feature (rcypher::cli): password/PIN prompts + direct-to-tty secret output (prompt.rs), the zxcvbn strength gate (strength.rs), and the policy-unlock prompt loop (unlock.rs: prompt_until_unlocked). Kept indicatif out of the lib via a pluggable UnlockProgress trait (+ NoProgress); the CLI supplies a SpinnerProgress adapter. rcypher-cli is now a thin shell: dropped its rpassword/zxcvbn deps (now via the feature), repointed imports, and collect_passwords delegates to the lib loop (keeping the --insecure-password test one-shot). cli feature pulls rpassword+zxcvbn+nix/fs. CI: added a clippy guard for the cli-without-fido2 path and excluded src/cli/* from coverage. All feature combos build/clippy/doc/test clean (104 tests). Task complete.

Done. Re-verified every narrowing against the current code (callers shifted after encrypt-factor-names/cli-reusable) and applied the crypto ones: Cypher::version + encrypt_with_aad + decrypt_with_aad pub -> pub(crate) (callers only auth/* + storage/value.rs + internal); EncryptionKey::as_bytes/hmac_key/salt/version pub -> pub(super) (only cipher.rs); LimitedReader and RingBuffer (+ their methods) pub -> pub(super), dropped the `pub use utils::LimitedReader` re-export in crypto/mod.rs and switched cipher.rs to `use super::utils::LimitedReader`. Re-check items: is_factor_id_char (now is_factor_name_char), probe_u16_tag, and the cfg(test) Leaf/PolicyNode re-exports all stay pub — they are cross-module but crate-internal (private auth/version modules, not in the public API), and redundant_pub_crate forbids pub(crate) there; the cfg(test) re-exports are still used by container tests. Verified: redundant_pub_crate did NOT fire on the pub(crate) methods of the re-exported Cypher type (lint is re-export-aware). Whole workspace builds --all --all-features --all-targets; clippy clean across default/no-default/cli/fido2/cli-no-default; -D warnings (denies dead_code) confirms nothing dead; 104 tests pass. Task complete.

Done (option a, per the update note). Capture the terminal's cooked-mode termios at startup in main() with nix::sys::termios::tcgetattr(stdin) (None if stdin isn't a tty, e.g. piped tests), move the Option<Termios> into start_security_timer's thread, and call a new restore_terminal() — tcsetattr(stdin, TCSANOW, &termios) + a newline — right before each of the timer thread's three process::exit calls (frozen clock, debugger detected, idle timeout). Those exits skip rustyline's Editor::Drop, which is what left the shell in raw mode; the in-loop bail!() paths were already fine (Editor drops on scope exit). The two startup process::exit calls (ptrace/debugger pre-checks) run before raw mode and need no restore. Added nix `term` feature to rcypher-cli. No global, no atexit; portable across Linux+macOS. Build/clippy(--all-targets + no-default)/fmt clean; 104 tests pass (PTY interactive tests confirm no startup regression). Manual confirmation of the actual restore: run `rcypher --insecure-allow-debugging <file>` and attach a debugger from another terminal (timer trips within ~1s), or let the 300s idle timeout fire at the prompt — the shell must come back with echo on. Task complete.

Policy changes currently re-wrap a stable DEK, so an older weaker snapshot can recover the key and decrypt current data. Add an atomic unlocked-container operation that creates a fresh DEK, rekeys all DataContainer inner values, re-encrypts factor names and auth-KEK bridges, redistributes shares under the new policy, verifies the result, and only then commits state. Use it for policy changes and factor removal; add regression tests proving old snapshots cannot decrypt post-change values.
title: Rotate DEK when tightening access policy or removing factors

notes: The full-file decrypt CLI currently creates/truncates the destination before input authentication and inherits umask permissions. Refactor to a same-directory 0600 temporary output, decrypt/authenticate completely, flush/sync, then atomically persist; reject input/output aliasing and add tests for wrong-password preservation and private permissions.
title: Write decrypted files privately and atomically

notes: Keep comfortable visible editing for long manually typed secrets, but remove VALUE from the main command line and rustyline history. Implement put KEY followed by a separate echoed secret prompt whose buffer is zeroized and never added to history; preserve compatibility only if it can be made equally safe. Add PTY/CLI tests.
title: Read put secrets with echo but exclude them from history

notes: Gate insecure-password, insecure-stdout, insecure-allow-debugging, and quiet testing escape hatches plus their behavior behind debug assertions so release binaries cannot select minimal Argon2, expose secrets through stdout, or disable debugger protection. Keep tests working in debug builds and add release-build surface validation where practical.
title: Compile unsafe test-only CLI options out of release builds

notes: RustSec RUSTSEC-2025-0141 marks bincode unmaintained. Design a new versioned container format using a maintained bounded serialization format, retain read-only compatibility for v7/v8, enforce explicit size/depth limits before allocation, write only the new version, document the migration, and add compatibility/malformed-input tests.
title: Migrate persisted metadata away from unmaintained bincode

notes: Update the resolved direct rand dependency from 0.9.2 to a patched compatible release (at least 0.9.3), confirm OsRng call sites remain correct, and run all feature configurations plus cargo audit.
title: Upgrade rand past RUSTSEC-2026-0097

notes: Add a pinned cargo-audit installation and cargo audit step to CI. Treat vulnerability and unsoundness advisories as failures; decide explicitly how to handle the bincode unmaintained warning while the migration task remains open, without suppressing unrelated future warnings.
title: Add RustSec cargo audit gate to CI

notes: Implemented fresh-DEK policy transitions through an encoded data clone; added old-snapshot regression coverage and updated the normative protocol.
Implemented private same-directory temporary output, input/output alias rejection, sync-before-persist, atomic replacement, and permission/preservation tests.
Implemented put KEY followed by a separate echoed rustyline value prompt; automatic history is disabled and only non-put commands are added manually. Updated CLI and PTY tests.
Gated all three insecure test escape hatches with cfg(debug_assertions); release verification remains to run.
Cargo.toml now requires rand 0.9.3+ and Cargo.lock resolves 0.9.4; the strict RustSec audit passes.
CI installs pinned cargo-audit 0.22.2 and runs with --deny warnings, explicitly ignoring only RUSTSEC-2025-0141 while bincode-migration remains open.

V8 must parse cleartext unlock metadata before the DEK can authenticate it. Add pre-authentication limits for total file/header size, factor count, credential/id/wrapped-blob lengths, policy nodes/depth, and Argon2 memory/time/parallelism. Reject values outside a supported security floor and resource ceiling before allocation or KDF execution; add malformed-file tests. This protects availability only—the payload AAD already prevents a modified header from decrypting.
title: Bound unauthenticated V8 metadata and Argon2 work

notes: Verified by old-snapshot outer-payload rejection and SecretStore full-history rekey regression tests; full all-feature and feature-matrix suites pass.
Verified destination remains intact on authentication failure and successful plaintext output is mode 0600 on Unix.
Verified updated piped CLI and PTY flows; all put commands are excluded from history, including rejected legacy inline-value syntax.
Verified optimized binaries reject insecure-password, insecure-stdout, and insecure-allow-debugging as unknown arguments; release all-feature check passes.
Resolved rand 0.9.4; strict cargo audit now passes with only the explicitly tracked bincode advisory ignored.
Local execution of the exact CI audit command passes.
Follow-up: retain a reconstructed canonical put KEY history entry after validation, while excluding raw/malformed put input and all prompted values.
Canonical validated put KEY commands are now retained; malformed inline puts remain excluded. Regression test added.
Refactor requested: restore the generic REPL loop by moving put-specific prompting, history handling, and prompt control flow behind command processing.
Refactored: run is command-agnostic; process_line owns history/display policy, process_cmd owns put-specific prompting and returns generic Continue/Exit control. Full tests and Clippy pass.
Second refactor requested: command match arms return their history representation; consolidate put parsing, prompting, execution, and canonical history policy in its command handler.

Completed: process_cmd arms return ProcessedCommand with command-owned history; global put/history special cases removed. cmd_put owns parsing, prompting, mutation, and canonical put KEY history. Failed commands are not recorded. Full workspace tests and Clippy pass.
Final consistency refactor: remove unnecessary PutCommand and make put match arm follow the same parse/call-handler/return-history structure as other commands.

Removed PutCommand. The put arm now follows the same structure as other arms: parse, prompt, invoke Result<()> cmd_put, and return canonical history. Added PTY regression proving history replays put KEY rather than the value.
Released 0.3.0 on 2026-06-28: rcypher and rcypher-cli published per docs/crate_release_process.md and release.yml built the binaries.

Follow-up from the code review of commit 7e51f18 (security hardening). Extracted file_io::persist_atomically: a private 0600 same-directory temp -> flush -> fsync contents -> rename -> fsync the directory entry, so a crash leaves either the previous file or the complete new one (never truncated/empty). Routed store saves, .bak backups, and full-file encrypt/decrypt through it. Merged run_encrypt/run_decrypt into one run_file_transform parameterized by a FileOp enum; a symlinked --output is resolved to its real target and written *through* (not replaced), in-place input/output aliasing is rejected, and output is 0600. Dropped the now-redundant exclusive flock (atomic rename supersedes it), the tempfile CLI dependency, and the nix "fs" feature.
title: Make all file writes durable and unify the atomic-write path

notes: Follow-up from the code review of commit 7e51f18. The put value is now read on a dedicated throwaway rustyline editor with empty history and its own kill ring, so it has no history navigation and a fragment killed mid-entry (Ctrl-U/W/K) cannot be yanked into a later command via the session editor (rustyline 13 never clears KillRing.slots on reset). Interactive command arguments are split on any run of spaces/tabs as a single delimiter. Failed *known* commands (e.g. get for a missing key) are recorded in history again so Up recalls them for correction; put still records only the canonical "put KEY", and unknown commands plus malformed inline-value put are never recorded. Added PTY and CLI regression tests.
title: Isolate put value entry and restore failed-command history recall

notes: This harness auto-injects CLAUDE.md but not AGENTS.md, so the taska/`ta` conventions in AGENTS.md were not surfaced at session start (an agent improvised against the .taska store instead of using `ta`). Add a one-line CLAUDE.md that imports AGENTS.md via the `@AGENTS.md` directive, keeping AGENTS.md as the single tool-agnostic source of truth (no duplication, no symlink portability issues on Windows checkouts).
title: Add CLAUDE.md importing AGENTS.md so Claude Code auto-loads repo guidance

notes: The lib exposes prompt_until_unlocked for opening an existing store but no creation-side counterpart, so every CLI reinvents rcypher-cli's create_store + offer_fido2_enrollment loop. Add cli::prompt_until_initialized<T: DataContainer>(data, &NewStoreConfig, &Argon2Params, &mut dyn UnlockProgress) -> Result<UnlockedContainer<T>>, mirroring the unlock side: show the unrecoverable-password warning, prompt+confirm a primary password (re-prompt while a weak one is declined), derive its key bracketed by UnlockProgress, and — with the fido2 feature and config.offer_fido2 — offer to enrol a security key and pick an AND/OR policy. Returns the unlocked container without saving (caller owns the path). NewStoreConfig carries the opinionated knobs (primary_factor_name, offer_fido2, fido2_rp_id, fido2_factor_name). Refactor rcypher-cli's create_store to call it (keeping the --insecure-password non-interactive path). Note: real-hardware FIDO2 path can't be exercised in the sandbox; cover the password-only flow with a PTY test and verify both feature configs build+clippy.
title: Add a reusable interactive new-store init flow to the cli module

notes: Refined per user: parameterize by which factor kinds to enrol (NewStoreConfig.factors = ordered &[InitFactor{kind,name}], non-empty; InitFactorKind::Fido2 gated on the feature), supporting FIDO2-only and password+FIDO2. Bootstrap the store from factors[0], enrol the rest, then let the end user choose the policy (AND-all vs OR-any via read_tty_confirmation for 2+ factors; richer policies via the existing `auth policy set`). FIDO2-only requires a new core primitive: add PolicyVault::create_with_fido2 + UnlockedContainer::create_with_fido2 (mirrors create_with_params but bootstraps the DEK + single-leaf policy from a key's hmac-secret; unconditional like enroll_fido2, unit-testable from synthetic bytes). prompt_until_initialized returns the unlocked container without saving.

Done. Added (lib): PolicyVault::create_with_fido2 + UnlockedContainer::create_with_fido2 (bootstrap a store from a key; unit-tested from synthetic hmac-secret, no hardware); fido2::DEFAULT_RP_ID; cli::{InitFactor, InitFactorKind, NewStoreConfig, prompt_until_initialized}. NewStoreConfig.fido2_rp_id is Option<&str> defaulting to DEFAULT_RP_ID (None recommended). Refactored rcypher-cli create_store to a thin dispatcher + create_store_interactively that builds the factor list (password + optional FIDO2 via the existing y/N) and calls prompt_until_initialized; the insecure-password test path stays direct. CLI FIDO2_RP_ID now references the lib default (single source); added FIDO2_FACTOR_NAME. PTY test covers the interactive password-only create. Note: too_many_arguments allowed on the 8-arg container create_with_fido2; the FIDO2-touch race for unlock was separately dropped (see unlock-prompts-stay-sequential memory). Verified: fmt, clippy (all-features + cli-no-fido2 + exact CI), --all-features doc, full workspace tests, core no-default tests, release build.

API-cleanup follow-up (user-requested): renamed UnlockedContainer::create_with_params -> create_with_password and PolicyVault::create -> create_with_password (paired with create_with_fido2); decoupled Argon2 from the container — enroll_password now takes the cost per-call and the UnlockedContainer.argon2 field is removed, so create_with_fido2 no longer takes argon2 (LockedContainer keeps its load-time argon2 for legacy unlock/upgrade). Renamed the init helper enroll_fido2_credential -> register_fido2_credential (it registers a device credential, not a store factor). Removed the duplicate cli::FIDO2_RP_ID; the CLI now uses rcypher::fido2::DEFAULT_RP_ID directly and InteractiveCli carries the session's enroll Argon2 cost. Updated the custom_format example + CHANGELOG (breaking API note). All green: fmt, clippy (all-features + both no-default variants), --all-features doc, full workspace + core no-default tests.

UX fix (user found by running): the FIDO2 enrollment is now OFFERED by the library AFTER the password bootstrap (not asked by the CLI before the password), and a missing/failing authenticator is caught and skipped (password-only) instead of aborting creation. prompt_until_initialized: first factor bootstraps (required); each remaining factor is offered ([y/N]); fido2 offer (offer_fido2) catches register errors and continues; policy is chosen over only the actually-enrolled factors. register_fido2_credential no longer prints a premature 'Enrolling' line. rcypher-cli drops its own pre-password FIDO2 gate and just lists [password, fido2]. PTY test reordered (password first, then key offer).
